data.title: Volvo Car AB (publ.) reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Volvo Car AB (publ.) press release (OTCPK:VLVOF): Q4 GAAP EPS was SEK 0.84 (SEK 1.04 in Q4 2023)
  • Revenue was SEK 112.1 bn (SEK 109.4 bn SEK in Q4 2023)
  • Q4 operating income (excl. JVs and associates) was SEK 6.3 bn (SEK 6.7 bn in Q4 2023)
  • Q4 operating income was SEK 3.9 bn SEK (SEK 5.4 bn in Q4 2023)
  • Q4 EBIT margin (excl. JVs and associates) was 5.6 per cent (6.1 per cent in Q4 2023)
  • Q4 EBIT margin was 3.4 per cent (4.9 per cent in Q4 2023)

data.title: DeepSeek being blocked by South Korean ministries on security risk - report
data.description:
The South Korean industry ministry has temporarily blocked staff from accessing Chinese AI model DeepSeek citing security risk, Reuters reported.

The government issued a notice on Tuesday directing ministries and agencies to apply caution about using AI services including DeepSeek and ChatGPT at work, the report added, citing ministry officials.

Government-operated Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power said it had blocked use of AI services, including DeepSeek earlier this month. The defense ministry has also blocked access to DeepSeek on its computers, which are for military use, the report noted.

The country's foreign ministry also halted access to the Chinese startup's AI service in computers that connect to external networks, the report added citing Yonhap News Agency.

It was not clear if the ministries had taken any actions against ChatGPT, developed by Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI.

DeepSeek and OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment by Seeking Alpha.

Earlier this week, the news agency reported that South Korea's Personal Information Protection Commission plans to ask DeepSeek about how the personal information of users is managed.

The ban In South Korea comes on the heels of Taiwan and Australia banning DeepSeek from all government devices over security concerns.

Last week, Italy's data protection authority Garante requested information from DeepSeek on the use and storage of personal data. Then, the regulator ordered the companies that provide the DeepSeek chatbot service to block the AI service in the country after they provided insufficient information, according to a report from Reuters.

Tech stocks plunged on Jan. 27, after DeepSeek last month unveiled its AI model R1 and detailed in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision. Nvidia (NVDA) saw around $589B in market cap erased on Jan. 27.

South Korean tech companies, including SK hynix and Naver, are now being more careful about using generative AI, the report added.

data.title: Yum China raises dividend by 50% to $0.24
data.description:
• Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) declares $0.24/share quarterly dividend, 50% increase from prior dividend of $0.16.

  • See YUMC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Euro Area retail sales rise 1.9% Y/Y in December
data.description:
Retail sales in the Euro Area decreased 0.20% in December 2024 over the previous month.

Retail sales increased 1.90% in December over the same month in the previous year.

In the Euro Area in December 2024, compared with December 2023, the volume of retail trade increased for food, drinks, tobacco by 0.2%; increased for non-food products (except automotive fuel) by 3.1%; increased for automotive fuel in specialized stores by 1.2%.

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Nikon reports results for nine months ended December 31, 2024
data.description:
• Nikon Corporation press release (OTCPK:NINOY): Revenue decreased 3.1% year-on-year to 512,671 million yen during the nine months ended December 31, 2024.
  • Operating profit decreased 26,328 million yen (76.4%) year-on-year to 8,110 million yen.
  • Profit attributable to owners of parent decreased 18,711 million yen (74.9%) year-on-year to 6,262 million yen.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: QCOM, F, and more
data.description:
Stock futures ticked up early Thursday as markets digested a flurry of corporate earnings, economic reports, and escalating global trade tensions.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Shares of Digital Turbine (NASDAQ:APPS) surged over 25% after the company topped FQ3 estimates and raised its full-year outlook. For fiscal 2025, the company now expects revenue of $485M–$490M, exceeding the $477.05M consensus, and non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA of $69M–$71M, reflecting nearly 50% growth.

Biggest stock losers

  • Shares of Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) dropped nearly 4% despite exceeding FQ1 expectations, as a weak outlook for its patent licensing business overshadowed stronger-than-expected guidance for sales and profits. For FQ2, the company forecasted adjusted EPS of $2.70–$2.90 (midpoint: $2.80), above the $2.69 consensus, while revenue is expected between $10.3B and $11.2B (midpoint: $10.75B), also exceeding estimates of $10.35B.
  • Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) plunged 25% after reporting an 11% revenue decline Y/Y and forecasting a mid-to-high teens sequential drop in sales from its largest segment for the current quarter. For FQ2 2025, the company projected revenue of $935M–$965M (midpoint: $950M), slightly above the $941.15M consensus, and adjusted EPS of $1.20, topping estimates of $1.19. Weak iPhone demand in China and inventory clearing by automotive and industrial customers weighed on results. Additionally, Skyworks appointed Philip Brace as president and CEO, effective February 17.
  • Ford Motor (NYSE:F) shares fell 5% after issuing weak 2025 guidance, as ongoing losses in its EV division overshadowed a Q4 earnings beat and strong ICE growth. The company expects its EV segment to post a $5.0B–$5.5B loss in FY25, while forecasting positive EBIT of $7.5B–$8.0B from Ford Pro and $3.5B–$4.0B from Ford Blue, with total adjusted EBIT between $7.0B and $8.5B. For Q1, the automaker expects adjusted EBIT to break even due to lower wholesale volumes and an unfavorable mix. Meanwhile, Ford appointed Sherry House as CFO, effective February 6, 2025, succeeding John Lawler, who remains Vice Chair.

data.title: Carlsberg reports FY24 revenue growth of 1.9% to DKK 75,011M
data.description:
• Carlsberg press release (OTCPK:CABGY): FY adjusted earnings per share for continuing operations of DKK 54.9 (+0.6%).
  • Reported revenue growth of 1.9% to DKK 75,011M.
  • Operating margin improvement of 10bp to 15.2%.
  • FY net profit of DKK 9,116M, including the reversal of impairment of DKK 2,258M from the disposal of the Russian business.

data.title: China files WTO complaint over Trump's 'discriminatory' tariffs, de minimis move
data.description:
China has officially filed a complaint with the World Trade Organization over U.S. President Donald Trump's new 10% tariff on Chinese goods and cancellation of the de minimis exemption for low-cost shipments, calling the measures "discriminatory and protectionist."

"The measures at issue, which are imposed on the basis of unfounded and false allegations concerning China, apply only to all products of Chinese origin," the complaint read. "The measures not only violate WTO rules, but are discriminatory and protectionist in nature."

China has called for consultations with the U.S. government regarding the tariffs. "China reserves the right to raise additional measures and claims regarding the matters identified herein during the course of consultations," according to the complaint.

Dispute settlements are carried out by the WTO's Appellate Body. But it has been effectively paralyzed since 2019, when Trump's first administration blocked new judge appointments to the body, claiming judicial overreach. The Biden administration maintained the block.

China's WTO complaint is not the only way it's hitting back at the U.S. Beijing plans to impose tariffs on U.S. coal, LNG, crude oil, agricultural machinery, and some large cars. It is also investigating Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), and designated Calvin Klein owner PVH (PVH) and biotech firm Illumina (ILMN) as "unreliable" entities.

"Beijing is unwilling to make concessions to the U.S. without more clarity on what Trump will put on the table – or take off the table in terms of future tariff threats," 22V Research's Michael Hirson said. "We do not see much near-term risk of escalation, but it seems less likely now that the two sides will climb down from their recent tariff hikes."
